title: Data Import Mapping Sheet
text:
Data Import Mapping Sheet for Acme Manufacturing

Prepared by: John Smith, Account Executive
Date: November 10, 2025

ROI Analysis:
- Current manual data entry time: 15 hours/week across 5 team members
- Projected time savings with automation: 12 hours/week (80% reduction)
- Annual cost savings estimate: $46,800 (based on $60/hour fully loaded cost)
- Expected productivity gain: 3,120 hours/year redirected to customer engagement

Data Migration Requirements:
- Legacy CRM export: 12,500 contact records
- Historical activities: 45,000 entries spanning 3 years
- Custom fields to map: 23 fields including industry codes, revenue bands, and territory assignments
- Data cleansing needed: ~8% duplicate contacts identified

Timeline:
- Week 1-2: Field mapping and validation rules setup
- Week 3: Test migration with 10% sample
- Week 4: Full migration and parallel run
- Week 5: Go-live and training

Special Terms:

- Price lock guarantee for renewal period

title: Implementation Timeline
text:
Beta Tech Solutions - Implementation Plan

Project Kickoff: December 15, 2025
Target Go-Live: January 31, 2026

Phase 1 - Foundation (Week 1-2):
- Account setup and SSO configuration
- User provisioning for 75 initial users
- Basic workflow configuration
- Admin training (4 hours)

Phase 2 - Data Migration (Week 3-4):
- Export from Salesforce (estimated 28,000 contacts)
- Data cleansing and deduplication
- Historical opportunity import (850 records)
- Activity history migration (18 months)

Phase 3 - Customization (Week 5-6):
- Custom fields: 18 fields including tech stack, company size tier
- Pipeline stages: 7 stages with probability mapping
- Email templates: 12 templates for sales sequences
- Dashboard configuration: 5 role-based dashboards

Phase 4 - Training & Launch (Week 7):
- End-user training sessions (3 sessions, 2 hours each)
- Manager training on reporting
- Go-live support with dedicated CSM

Pricing Summary:
- Annual subscription (75 users): $28,000
- One-time implementation fee: $4,000
- Total first year investment: $32,000

title: Statement of Work - Analytics Add-on
text:
Statement of Work: Crown Foods Analytics Implementation

Project Overview:
Implementation of Advanced Analytics module for Crown Foods distribution network tracking.

Scope of Work:
1. Dashboard Development:
   - Regional sales performance dashboard
   - Distributor scorecard with YoY comparisons
   - Product mix analysis by territory
   - Weekly automated report distribution to 12 stakeholders

2. Data Integration:
   - Connect to Crown Foods ERP (SAP) for order data
   - Daily sync of shipment and inventory levels
   - Historical data import: 24 months of sales data

3. Custom Metrics:
   - Distributor health score (composite of 8 factors)
   - Territory coverage index
   - Seasonal demand forecasting model

Timeline: 6 weeks from contract signature
Investment: $4,800 one-time setup + $1,800/year subscription

Success Criteria:
- All dashboards operational within 6 weeks
- 95% data accuracy on automated syncs
- User training completed for 8 regional managers

title: Security Questionnaire Response
text:
Security Questionnaire - Delta Health Clinics

Completed: August 15, 2025
Reviewed by: Delta Health IT Security Team

1. Data Encryption:
   Q: Is data encrypted at rest and in transit?
   A: Yes. AES-256 encryption at rest, TLS 1.3 for all data in transit.

2. Access Controls:
   Q: What authentication methods are supported?
   A: SAML 2.0 SSO, OAuth 2.0, MFA via authenticator apps or SMS.

3. HIPAA Compliance:
   Q: Is the platform HIPAA compliant?
   A: Yes. BAA available. Annual third-party audits. SOC 2 Type II certified.

4. Data Residency:
   Q: Where is data stored?
   A: US data centers only (AWS us-east-1, us-west-2). No offshore processing.

5. Backup & Recovery:
   Q: What is the backup policy?
   A: Daily automated backups, 30-day retention, 4-hour RTO, 1-hour RPO.

6. Incident Response:
   Q: What is the breach notification timeline?
   A: 24-hour notification for confirmed breaches per BAA terms.

7. User Activity Logging:
   Q: Is user activity tracked?
   A: Full audit logs retained 7 years. Exportable for compliance reviews.

Status: APPROVED for use with PHI data
Next Review: August 2026

title: Trial Success Criteria
text:
Eastern Travel Co. - Trial Evaluation Criteria

Trial Period: January 5 - February 5, 2025

Success Metrics (must achieve 3 of 5 to proceed):

1. User Adoption:

2. Data Quality:
   Target: 500+ contacts imported and enriched
   Measurement: Contact completeness score

3. Pipeline Visibility:
   Target: All active opportunities tracked ($2M+ pipeline)
   Measurement: Pipeline coverage percentage

4. Activity Logging:
   Target: 100+ activities logged per week
   Measurement: Calls, emails, meetings recorded

5. User Satisfaction:
   Target: NPS score of 30+
   Measurement: End-of-trial survey

Trial Support Included:
- Dedicated CSM: Maya Malik
- Weekly check-in calls (Tuesdays 2pm)
- Slack channel for real-time support
- 2 training sessions included

Conversion Offer:
- Sign by Feb 15: 15% discount on first year
- Annual commitment: $8,000 (normally $9,400)

title: Executive Summary - Harbor Logistics
text:
Executive Summary: Harbor Logistics CRM Opportunity

Prepared for: Harbor Logistics Leadership Team
Date: November 10, 2025

Company Overview:
- Industry: Third-party logistics (3PL)
- Employees: 450
- Revenue: $85M annually
- Locations: 12 warehouses across US West Coast

Current Challenges:
1. Disconnected customer data across 3 systems
2. No unified view of shipper relationships
3. Manual reporting consuming 20+ hours/week
4. Lost opportunities due to poor lead follow-up (estimated $500K/year)

Proposed Solution:
- Acme CRM Professional (60 users)
- Custom logistics workflow module
- ERP integration (NetSuite connector)
- Mobile app for field sales team

Expected Outcomes:
- 360-degree customer view across all touchpoints
- 50% reduction in reporting time
- 25% improvement in lead response time
- Projected ROI: 280% over 3 years

Investment:
- Year 1: $26,000 (includes implementation)
- Year 2+: $22,000/year
- Total 3-year TCO: $70,000

Risks & Mitigations:
- Risk: User adoption in warehouses
- Mitigation: Phased rollout, mobile-first training

Decision Timeline: Q4 2025

title: ROI Analysis - Harbor Logistics
text:
ROI Analysis: Harbor Logistics CRM Investment

Prepared by: Maya Malik, Solutions Consultant
Date: November 10, 2025

Current State Costs (Annual):
- Manual data entry across systems: $48,000 (800 hours × $60/hr)
- Duplicate outreach due to data issues: $24,000 (lost productivity)
- Missed follow-ups (est. lost deals): $500,000
- Report generation: $31,200 (520 hours × $60/hr)
- Total inefficiency cost: $603,200/year

Projected Savings with Acme CRM:

Year 1:
- Data entry reduction (60%): $28,800
- Eliminated duplicates: $24,000
- Improved close rate (+5%): $125,000
- Reporting automation (80%): $24,960
- Total Year 1 Savings: $202,760

- Net Year 1 Benefit: $176,760

Year 2-3:
- Ongoing savings: $220,000/year (process maturity)
- Investment: $22,000/year
- Net Annual Benefit: $198,000

3-Year Summary:
- Total Investment: $70,000
- Total Savings: $642,760
- Net Benefit: $572,760
- ROI: 818%
- Payback Period: 2.1 months

Assumptions:
- 85% user adoption by month 3
- 5% improvement in win rate (conservative)
- Fully loaded labor cost: $60/hour

title: Initial upgrade conversation
text:
Call with Anna Chen (VP Sales) - October 5, 2025, 3:30 PM
Duration: 45 minutes

Discussion Summary:
Anna expressed interest in upgrading from Professional to Enterprise tier. Main drivers:
1. Need for advanced forecasting - current reports are manual and take her team 6 hours/week
2. Want API access to connect with their BI tool (Tableau)
3. Interested in the AI-powered lead scoring feature

Current Pain Points Mentioned:
- Sales reps spending too much time on data entry
- No visibility into email engagement metrics
- Difficulty tracking which campaigns drive pipeline

Budget Discussion:
- Anna has budget authority up to $25K without CFO approval
- Fiscal year ends March 31, would prefer to close in Q4
- Mentioned competitor (HubSpot) quoted them $28K for similar features

Next Steps Agreed:
1. Send proposal with Enterprise pricing by Oct 8
2. Schedule demo of forecasting module for her team
3. Provide customer references in manufacturing sector

Follow-up: Demo scheduled for Oct 20 with Anna + 3 sales managers

title: Demo and Q&A session
text:
Meeting: Enterprise Features Demo - October 20, 2025, 2:00 PM
Duration: 90 minutes
Attendees: Anna Chen (VP Sales), Mike Torres, Sarah Kim, David Park (Sales Managers)

Demo Agenda Covered:
1. Advanced Forecasting Module (30 min)
   - AI-driven pipeline predictions
   - Scenario modeling (best/worst/likely)
   - Manager override capabilities
   - Reaction: Very positive, Sarah asked about accuracy rates

2. Custom Reporting & Dashboards (25 min)
   - Drag-and-drop report builder
   - Scheduled report delivery
   - Export to Tableau/PowerBI
   - Reaction: Mike excited about eliminating manual Excel work

3. Lead Scoring & Routing (20 min)
   - ML-based scoring model
   - Auto-assignment rules
   - Score decay over time
   - Reaction: David concerned about learning curve

Questions Raised:
- Q: How long to implement? A: 2-3 weeks for full setup
- Q: Can we keep current workflows? A: Yes, additive not replacement
- Q: Training included? A: 4 hours admin, 2 hours end-user

Concerns:
- David worried about change management with his team
- Anna wants to see it working before committing

Next Steps:
- Offer 2-week pilot with forecasting module
- Send training video library
- Schedule follow-up with Anna Nov 5

Internal Note - November 10, 2025
Author: John Smith

Account Health Assessment: YELLOW (Caution)

Positive Indicators:
- Strong usage metrics (94% adoption)
- Executive sponsor engaged (Anna)
- Budget available for upgrade
- Competitive situation manageable

Concerns:

- Heard from industry contact that they're also talking to Salesforce

Risk Assessment:

Recommended Actions:

2. Offer executive briefing with our VP Sales
3. Accelerate the pilot program - get them using new features ASAP
4. Loop in CS team for proactive outreach

Escalation: Flagging to sales leadership in weekly pipeline review.

title: Initial scoping call
text:
Call with Lisa Wong (Head of Sales Ops) - September 1, 2025, 10:00 AM
Duration: 60 minutes

Background:
Beta Tech is a B2B SaaS company, 200 employees, selling developer tools. Currently using Salesforce but frustrated with complexity and cost.

Requirements Discussed:
1. User count: 75 sales + 25 marketing = 100 users
2. Must-have integrations: Slack, Gmail, Zoom, Outreach
3. Data migration: 28,000 contacts, 850 opportunities, 18 months of activities

Pain Points with Current System:
- Salesforce admin requires dedicated FTE ($120K/year)
- Reps spend 2 hours/day on data entry
- Marketing has no visibility into sales activities
- Reporting takes 2 weeks to build anything custom

Budget:
- Current Salesforce spend: $85,000/year
- Target for new solution: Under $40,000/year

Decision Process:
- Lisa owns the evaluation
- Final sign-off: CRO (Omar Patel)
- Timeline: Decision by end of November
- Competitors in evaluation: Pipedrive, Close.com

Next Steps:
- Send integration documentation
- Schedule technical deep-dive with their IT team
- Provide Salesforce migration case study

Expansion Components:
- 15 additional user licenses (growing sales team)
- Advanced Analytics module for distributor tracking
- Custom dashboard development (included)

Key Stakeholder:
- Maria Santos (Director of Sales) - Decision maker for this deal
- Low risk: Very satisfied customer, no competitive evaluation

Year 1 Success Metrics:
- Email tracking heavily used
- Mobile app adopted by field sales
- Support satisfaction: High

Requested Improvements:
- More flexible reporting (addressed in Analytics add-on)
- Better territory management (on roadmap for Q2 2026)
- SAP integration improvements (shipping Q1 2026)

Risks:
- Very low risk deal
- Only concern: Budget timing around year-end

Next Steps:
1. Maria to confirm headcount by Dec 1
2. Demo Analytics module on Oct 25 (completed, well-received)
3. Send contract for signature by Dec 10
4. Create saved group for renewals and start outreach to users

title: Opportunity Notes – OPP-DELTA-RENEW
text:
Delta Health Clinics - Renewal Opportunity

Opportunity Details:
- Stage: Qualified
- Value: $22,000 USD (flat renewal)
- Expected Close: September 1, 2025 [PAST DUE]
- Probability: 85%

Account Context:
- 8 medical clinics in Midwest
- 30 users (clinic staff and management)
- Use case: Patient referral tracking, physician outreach

Key Stakeholder:
- Erin Walsh (Practice Manager) - Main contact and decision maker

Renewal Blocker (Resolved):
- Legal team required HIPAA compliance review
- Security questionnaire completed August 15
- All requirements satisfied (SOC 2, BAA available, encryption)

Usage Highlights:
- Referral tracking: 2,400 referrals logged YTD
- Physician database: 1,800 contacts maintained
- Task completion: 89% rate

Areas Needing Attention:
- Mobile app adoption only 15% (target was 50%)
- Email campaigns underutilized (only 3 all year)
- Still using Excel for some reports

Risks:
- Process risk: Legal review took longer than expected
- Adoption risk: Need mobile rollout plan

Next Steps:
1. Schedule 30-minute admin setup call for mobile training
2. Create mobile adoption program for clinic staff
3. Develop custom patient satisfaction tracking field
4. Follow up on contract signature (pending legal final review)

title: Opportunity Notes – OPP-EAST-TRIAL
text:
Eastern Travel Co. - Trial Conversion Opportunity

Opportunity Details:
- Stage: New (Trial in Progress)
- Value: $8,000 USD (10-user starter package)
- Expected Close: February 28, 2025
- Probability: 40%

Trial Details:
- Trial Period: January 5 - February 5, 2025
- Pilot Users: 10 (sales team)
- CSM: Maya Malik

Trial Success Criteria (must hit 3 of 5):
1. User adoption: 80% weekly active ✓ Target met
2. Data quality: 500+ contacts imported ✓ 612 imported
3. Pipeline coverage: All opportunities tracked ✓ $2.1M tracked
4. Activity logging: 100+ per week ⚠️ Only 67/week
5. User satisfaction: NPS 30+ ❓ Pending survey

Key Stakeholder:
- Sanjay Gupta (Sales Director) - Trial owner
- Note: CEO is watching this pilot closely

Current Status:
- 3 of 4 measurable criteria met
- Activity logging below target - reps not forming habit
- Overall engagement positive

Risks:
- Low activity logging could signal adoption issues at scale
- CEO scrutiny means high bar for success
- Short timeline to decision

Conversion Incentive:
- 15% discount if signed by Feb 15
- Annual price: $8,000 (normally $9,400)

Potential Expansion:
- If pilot succeeds, looking at 40 users ($16K ARR)

Next Steps:
1. Schedule 30-minute admin setup call to boost activity logging
2. Send NPS survey by Jan 30
3. Prepare conversion proposal for Feb 5 meeting

title: Opportunity Notes – OPP-FUSION-UPSELL
text:
Fusion Retail Group - Expansion Opportunity

Opportunity Details:
- Stage: Qualified
- Value: $12,000 USD (40 users at volume discount)
- Expected Close: May 10, 2026
- Probability: 70%

Expansion Context:
- Current: 25 users (sales only), $10K/year
- Proposed: 40 users (sales + marketing), $12K/year
- Net new: 15 marketing seats, $4K incremental

Key Stakeholder:
- Emma Davis (VP Marketing) - Expansion champion
- Currently using Mailchimp with no CRM visibility

Marketing Team Use Cases:
1. Campaign attribution tracking
2. Lead scoring before sales handoff
3. Email nurture sequence management
4. Marketing-sourced pipeline reporting

Business Case:
- Sales complains about lead quality
- CMO can't prove marketing ROI
- Duplicate records between systems causing issues

Budget:
- Emma has $15K for tools this year
- $5K available for CRM expansion
- Needs to close before holiday campaigns (Nov)

Risks:
- Timeline tight for Nov implementation
- Need to coordinate with existing sales users
- Integration with Mailchimp required

Next Steps:
1. Demo marketing features to Emma's team
2. Create implementation plan for Nov 1 go-live
3. Coordinate with sales team on workflow changes
4. Create saved group for renewals and start outreach

title: Opportunity Notes – OPP-HARBOR-NEW
text:
Harbor Logistics - New Business Opportunity

Opportunity Details:
- Stage: Discovery
- Value: $26,000 USD (Year 1 with implementation)
- Expected Close: August 30, 2025 [PAST DUE - Delayed]
- Probability: 50%

Company Profile:
- Industry: Third-party logistics (3PL)
- Size: 450 employees, $85M revenue
- Locations: 12 warehouses, US West Coast
- Key customers: Amazon, Target, Wayfair

Deal Components:
- 60 users: $22,000/year
- Implementation: $4,000 one-time
- NetSuite integration: Included

Key Stakeholder:
- Jacob Torres (COO) - Decision maker
- CEO approval required for final sign-off

Why They're Evaluating:
- 3 disconnected systems today
- $85K/year on underutilized Salesforce
- 20+ hours/week on manual reporting
- Estimated $500K/year lost to poor lead follow-up

Competitive Situation:
- Evaluating: Acme CRM, Salesforce (renewal), Zoho
- Leaning away from Salesforce (cost and complexity)
- Our advantage: NetSuite integration, pricing, logistics focus

Risks:
- Deal slipped from August target
- CEO approval process unclear
- Large migration scope (complex data)

ROI Summary (Prepared Nov 10):
- 3-year savings: $642,760
- Investment: $70,000
- ROI: 818%
- Payback: 2.1 months

Next Steps:
1. Schedule CEO briefing
2. Create saved group for renewals and start outreach
3. Finalize NetSuite integration scope
4. Address warehouse user adoption concerns
